objective: Plan repeated useful AIIP work between two independently governed Systems with a provider or verifier independent of both System administrations, participant-specific surplus evaluation, full external-Worker lifecycle, portable exit, and a negative decline control.
falsifiable_claim: Repeated useful work can cross sovereignty without shared runtime, database, or administration, using a provider or verifier independent of both sovereign System administrations, while every required participant and sponsor clears its frozen baseline and a negative control declines then continues locally.
exit_criteria: Repeated useful work crosses sovereignty without shared runtime, database, or administration; a provider or verifier independent of both sovereign System administrations participates, and an external Worker completes the full lifecycle.
Every required party and sponsor clears frozen benefit baselines after declared costs and risks; the negative control declines and continues locally.
A retained negative fixture replaces the independent provider or verifier with one controlled by either sovereign System administration and the proof verifier rejects the run.
Retained evidence log contains exactly one literal M13_TWO_SOVEREIGN_SURPLUS_AND_DECLINE_PROOF_EXIT=0; process exit status alone is insufficient.
adversarial_or_fault_proof: Inject shared-admin dependence; replace the independent provider or verifier with one controlled by either sovereign System administration; and test terms drift, result or acceptance substitution, dispute suppression, portable-exit failure, cost/risk omission, subsidy hiding, failed baseline, and negative-control coercion.
remaining_nonclaims: No broad network effect, marketplace economics, L1 demand, universal surplus, or stage closure claim.
source_provenance: Plan-level proposal derived from docs/architecture/_meta/work-items/m0-m14-plan-gap-audit.md at origin/master 69592149186cb29383a397ad0aa3ad6f5ab4ab7b; status transaction 2026-07-22.
